title: scrophularia libanotica subsp. libanotica
intro: Scrophularia libanotica subsp. libanotica, also known as Mount Lebanon figwort, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and boosting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, Scrophularia libanotica subsp. libanotica is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
